basics.name: Marc Lundgren
basics.label: Front End Software Engineer
basics.summary: I'm a Front End Developer with 4.5 years of Front End Development experience. I studied Computer Science and mastered Google AdWords advertising part-time during college. I've worked on teams large and small, international and domestic, from startups to large organizations. I enjoy traveling the world with my wife whenever an opportunity arises. I live in Las Vegas, Nevada and feel most productive & happy when either working remote with a great team or from an office close to home. I'm currently studying React + Redux and working on a few pet projects in my free time.

work.summary: I learned how to build and maintain a high-traffic complex single page application using Ember.js and ember-data in a remote, agile start-up environment. I've worked on a React Native project to deliver a cloud-data backed personal identification app.

I learned to leverage React.js Backbone, and Underscore, as well as Moment.js while building a multi-source web calendar. Another project was a Video Gallery using Mithril.js (an FRP framework) and Lodash.
highlights: Single Page Application: Google Multi Calendar
Single Page Application: FIDM Video Gallery

work.summary: I developed and contributed to the Liferay Portal and AlloyUI projects open-source projects. My code contributions typically came in the form of JavaScript, CSS, Sass and JSP. Liferay UI Developers require knowledge of Bootstrap 2 and 3, as well as a deep understanding of YUI, an MVC framework similar to jQuery. Some of the tools and technology I learned to use at Liferay include Git, Sublime Text, Gulp, Grunt, Node, Ant, Bash/ZSH scripting, and MySQL.

references.name: Jane Kim
references.reference: Marc has passion and enthusiasm for what he does. After having worked with him for the past half year, I've come to see that personally. He has several traits that make him an asset for any company: excellent communication, team-oriented, proactive attitude, and innovative vision. It's easy to see the drive he has in making improvements to whatever he's working on, especially when discussing current projects in progress. There's an intensity and focus that any company would benefit from.  He has an attitude that I believe is especially great for start-ups: the hunger to be part of something bigger than himself, that will ultimately benefit a larger group. His want to constantly improve on a product -- to bring innovation to something that already exists -- and to go one step further in creating something completely new sets him apart from drones in larger corporations. Having worked with him even ignited a new spark in me to want to do something greater, and I respect him for that. If anyone's to be given a chance with a position, it's Marc.
